author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel of John vii. 6, etc., where Jesus said that He was not going up unto the feast, and notwithstanding went up.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel of John vii. 6, etc., where Jesus said that He was not going up unto the feast, and notwithstanding went up.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I Purpose by the Lord’s assistance to treat of this section of the Gospel which has just been read; nor is there a little difficulty here, lest the truth be endangered, and falsehood glory.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not that either the truth can perish, nor falsehood triumph.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now hearken for a while what difficulty this lesson has; and being made attentive by the propounding of the difficulty, pray that I may be sufficient for its solution.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Jews’ feast of tabernacles was at hand;” these it seems are the days which they observe even to this day, when they build huts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this solemnity of theirs is called from the building of tabernacles; since σκηνὴ means a “tabernacle,” σκηνοπηγία is the building of a tabernacle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These days were kept as feast days among the Jews; and it was called one feast day, not because it was over in one day, but because it was kept up by a continued festivity; just as the feast day of the Passover, and the feast day of unleavened bread, and notwithstanding, as is manifest, that feast is kept throughout many days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This anniversary then was at hand in Judæa, the Lord Jesus was in Galilee, where He had also been brought up, where too He had relations and kinsfolk, whom Scripture calls “His brethren.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “His brethren, therefore,” as we have heard it read, “said unto Him, Pass from hence, and go into Judæa; that Thy disciples also may see Thy works that Thou doest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For no man doeth anything in secret, and himself seeketh to be known openly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If Thou do these things, manifest Thyself to the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then the Evangelist subjoins, “For neither did His brethren believe in Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then they did not believe in Him, the words they threw out were of envy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Jesus answered them, My time is not yet come; but your time is alway ready.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The world cannot hate you; but Me it hateth, because I testify of it that the works thereof are evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Go ye up to this feast day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I go not up to this feast day, for My time is not yet accomplished.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then follows the Evangelist; “When He had said these words, He Himself stayed in Galilee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when His brethren were gone up, then went He also up to the feast day, not openly, but as it were in secret.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus far is the extent of the difficulty, all the rest is clear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is the difficulty? what makes the perplexity? what is in peril?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lest the Lord, yea, to speak more plainly, lest the Truth Itself should be thought to have lied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if we would have it thought that He lied, the weak will receive an authority for lying.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard say that He lied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For those who think that He lied, speak thus, “He said that He should not go up to the feast day, and He went up.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the first place then, let us, as far as in the press of time we can, see whether he does lie, who says a thing and does it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For example, I have told a friend, “I will see you to-morrow;” some greater necessity occurs to hinder me; I have not on that account spoken falsely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when I made the promise, I meant what I said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when some greater matter occurred, which hindered the accomplishment of my promise, I had no design to lie, but I was not able to fulfil the promise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, to my thinking I have used no labour to persuade you, but have merely suggested to your good sense, that he who promises something, and doeth it not, does not lie, if, that he do it not, something has occurred to hinder the fulfilment of his promise, not to be any proof of falsehood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But some one who hears me will say, “Canst thou then say this of Christ, that He either was not able to fulfil what He would, or that He did not know things to come?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou doest well, good is thy suggestion, right thy hint; but, O man, share with me my anxiety.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dare we to say that He lies, Who we do not dare to say is weak in power?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I for my part, to the best of my thinking, as far as according to my infirmity I am able to judge, would choose that a man should be deceived in any matter rather than lie in any.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to be deceived is the portion of infirmity, to lie of iniquity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou hatest, O Lord,” saith he, “all them that work iniquity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And immediately after, “Thou shalt destroy all them that speak a lie.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Either “iniquity” and “a lie” are upon a level; or, “Thou shalt destroy,” is more than “Thou hatest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he who is held in hatred, is not immediately punished by destruction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let that question be, whether there be ever a necessity to lie; for I am not now discussing that; it is a dark question, and has many lappings; I have not time to cut them, and to come to the quick.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore let the treatment of it be deferred to some other time; for peradventure it will be cured by the Divine assistance without any words of mine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But attend and distinguish between what I have deferred, and what I wish to treat of to-day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whether on any occasion one may lie, this difficult and most obscure question I defer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whether Christ lied, whether the Truth spake anything false, this, being reminded of it by the Gospel lesson, have I undertaken to-day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what the difference is between being deceived, and lying, I will briefly state.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is deceived who thinks what he says to be true, and therefore says it, because he thinks it true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if this which he that is deceived says, were true, he would not be deceived; if it were not only true, but he also knew it to be true, he would not lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is deceived then, in that it is false, and he thinks it true; but he only says it because he thinks it true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The error lies in human infirmity, not in the soundness of the conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whosoever thinks it to be false, and asserts it as true, he lies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, my Brethren, draw the distinction, ye who have been brought up in the Church, instructed in the Lord’s Scriptures, not uninformed, nor simple, nor ignorant men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there are among you men learned and erudite, and not indifferently instructed in all kinds of literature; and with those of you who have not learnt that literature which is called liberal, it is more that ye have been nourished up in the word of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If I labour in explaining what I mean, do ye aid me both by the attention of your hearing, and the thoughtfulness of your meditations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nor will ye aid, unless ye are aided.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore pray we mutually for one another, and look equally for our common Succour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is deceived, who whereas what he says is false, thinks it to be true; but he lies, who thinks a thing to be false, and gives it out as true, whether it be true or false.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Observe what I have added, “whether it be true or false;” yet he who thinks it to be false, and asserts it as true, lies; he aims to deceive.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what good is it to him, that it is true?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He all the while thinks it false, and says it as if it were true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What he says is true in itself, it is in itself true; with regard to him it is false, his conscience does not hold that which he is saying; he thinks in himself one thing to be true, he gives out another for truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His is a double heart, not single; he does not bring out that which he has in it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The double heart has long since been condemned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “With deceitful lips in a heart and a heart have they spoken evil things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Had it been enough to say, “in the heart have they spoken evil things,” where is the “deceitful lips”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is deceit?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When one thing is done, another pretended.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Deceitful lips are not a single heart; and because not a single heart, therefore “in a heart and a heart;” therefore “in a heart” twice, because the heart is double.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then think we of the Lord Jesus Christ, that He lied?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it is a less evil to be deceived than to lie, dare we to say that He lies who we dare not to say is deceived?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He is neither deceived, nor doth He lie; but in very deed as it is written (for of Him is it understood, of Him ought it to be understood), “Nothing false is said unto the King, and nothing false shall proceed out of His mouth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If by King here he meant any man, let us prefer Christ the King, to a man-king.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if, which is the truer understanding of it, it is Christ of whom he spake, if I say, as is the truer understanding of it, it is Christ of whom he spake (for to Him indeed nothing false is said, in that He is not deceived; from His Mouth nothing false proceedeth, in that He doth not lie); let us look how we are to understand the section of the Gospel, and let us not make the pitfall of a lie, as it were, on heavenly authority.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But it is most absurd to be seeking to explain the truth, and to prepare a place for a lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What art thou teaching me, I ask thee, who art explaining this text to me, what wouldest thou teach me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not know whether you would dare to say, “Falsehood.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if you should dare to say this, I turn away mine ears, and fasten them up with thorns, that if you should try to force your way, I might through their very pricking make away without the explanation of the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me what thou wouldest wish to teach me, and thou hast resolved the difficulty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me, I pray thee; lo, here I am; mine ears are open, my heart is ready, teach me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I ask, what?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will not travel through many things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What art thou going to teach me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatsoever learning thou art about to bring forward, whatsoever strength to show in disputation, tell me this one thing only, one of two things I ask; art thou going to teach me truth or falsehood?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do we suppose he will answer lest one depart; lest while he is open-mouthed and making an effort to bring out his words, I forthwith leave him: what will he promise but truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am listening, standing, expecting, most earnestly expecting.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See here, he who promised that he will teach me truth, insinuates falsehood concerning Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then shall he teach truth, who would say that Christ is false?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If Christ is false, can I hope that thou wilt tell me the truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does he say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hath Christ spoken falsely?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where, I ask thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Where He says, ‘I go not up to the feast day;’ and went up.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For my part, I should wish thoroughly to examine this place, if so be we may see that Christ did not speak falsely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea rather, seeing that I have no doubt that Christ did not speak falsely, I will either thoroughly examine this passage and understand it, or, not understanding it, I will defer it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet that Christ spoke falsely will I never say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Grant that I have not understood it; I will depart in my ignorance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For better is it with piety to be ignorant, than with madness to pronounce judgment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Notwithstanding we are trying to examine, if so be by His assistance, who is the Truth, we may find something, and be found something ourselves, and this something will not be in the Truth a lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if in searching I find a lie, I find not a something but a nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then look where it is thou sayest that Christ lied.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He will say, “In that He said, ‘I go not up to this feast,’ and went up.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence dost thou know that He said so?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What if I were to say, nay, not I, but any one, for God forbid that I should say it; what if another were to say, “Christ did not say this;” whereby dost thou refute him, whereby wilt thou prove it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wouldest open the book, find the passage, point it out to the man, yea with great confidence force the book upon him if he resisted, “Hold it, mark, read, it is the Gospel you have in your hands.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why, I ask thee, why dost thou so rudely accost this feeble one?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not be so eager; speak more composedly, more tranquilly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, it is the Gospel I have in my hands; and what is there in it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He answers: “The Gospel declares that Christ said what thou deniest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And wilt thou believe that Christ said it, because the Gospel declares it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Decidedly for that reason,” says he.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I marvel exceedingly how thou shouldest say that Christ lieth, and the Gospel doth not lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But lest haply when I speak of the Gospel, thou shouldest think of the book itself, and imagine the parchment and ink to be the Gospel, see what the Greek word means; Gospel is “a good messenger,” or “a good message.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The messenger then doth not lie, and doth He who sent him, lie?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This messenger, the Evangelist to wit, to give his name also, this John who wrote this, did he lie concerning Christ, or say the truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Choose which you will, I am ready to hear you on either side.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he spake falsely, you have no means of proving that Christ spake those words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he said the truth, truth cannot flow from the fountain of falsehood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is the Fountain?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ: let John be the stream.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The stream comes to me, and you say to me, “Drink securely;” yea, whereas you alarm me as to the Fountain Himself, whereas you tell me there is falsehood in the Fountain, you say to me, “Drink securely.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do I drink?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What said John, that Christ spake falsely?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence came John?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is he who came from Him, to tell me truth, when He from whom he came lied?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have read in the Gospel plainly, “John lay on the Lord’s Breast;” but I conclude that he drank in truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What saw he as he lay on the Lord’s Breast?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What drank he in? what, but that which he poured forth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Same was in the beginning with God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which was made in Him was life, and the Life was the Light of men; And the Light shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehended It not;” nevertheless It shineth, and though I chance to have some obscurity, and cannot thoroughly comprehend It, still It shineth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “There was a man sent from God, whose name was John; he came to bear witness of the Light, that all men through him might believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was not the Light:” who?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: John the Baptist.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of him saith John the Evangelist, “He was not the Light;” of whom the Lord saith, “He was a burning, and a shining lamp.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But a lamp can be lighted, and extinguished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then? whence drawest thou the distinction? of what place art thou enquiring?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He to whom the lamp bare witness, “was the True Light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where John added, “the True,” there art thou looking out for a lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But hear still the same Evangelist John pouring forth what he had drunk in; “And we beheld,” saith he, “His glory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What did he behold? what glory beheld he?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The glory as of the Only-Begotten of the Father, full of grace and truth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then, see, if we ought not haply to restrain weak or rash disputings, and to presume nothing false of the truth, to give to the Lord what is His due; let us give glory to the Fountain, that we may fill ourselves securely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Now God is true, but every man a liar.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God is full; every man is empty; if he will be filled, let him come to Him That is full.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come unto Him, and be enlightened.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Moreover, if man is empty, in that he is a liar, and he seeks to be filled, and with haste and eagerness runs to the fountain, he wishes to be filled, he is empty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou sayest, “Beware of the fountain, there is falsehood there.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What else sayest thou, but “there is poison there”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “You have already,” he says, “said all, already have you checked, already chastened me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But tell me how He did not speak falsely who said, ‘I go not up,’ and went up?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will tell you, if I can; but think it no little matter, that if I have not established you in the truth, I have yet kept you back from rashness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will nevertheless tell you, what I imagine you know even already, if you remember the words which I have set forth to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The words themselves solve the difficulty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That feast was kept for many days.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On this, that is this present feast day, saith He, this day, that is when they hoped, He went not up; but when He Himself resolved to go.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now mark what follows, “When He had said these words, He Himself stayed in Galilee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then He did not go up on that feast day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For His brethren wished that He should go first; therefore had they said, “Pass from hence into Judæa.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They did not say, “Let us pass,” as though they would be His companions; or, “Follow us into Judæa,” as though they would go first; but as though they would send Him before them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He wished that they should go before; He avoided this snare, impressing His infirmity as Man, hiding the Divinity; this He avoided, as when He fled into Egypt.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this was no effect of want of power, but even of truth, that He might give an example of caution; that no servant of His might say, “I do not fly, because it is disgraceful;” when haply it might be expedient to fly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As He was going to say to His disciples, “When they have persecuted you in this city, flee ye into another;” He gave them Himself this example.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He was apprehended, when He willed; He was born, when He willed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That they might not anticipate Him then, and announce that He was coming, and plots be prepared; He said, “I go not up to this feast day.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He said, “I go not up,” that He might be hid; He added “this,” that He might not lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Something He expressed, something He suppressed, something He repressed; yet said He nothing false, for “nothing false proceedeth out of His Mouth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Finally, after He had said these words, “When His brethren were gone up;” the Gospel declares it, attend, read what you have objected to me; see if the passage itself do not solve the difficulty, see if I have taken from anywhere else what to say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then the Lord was waiting for, that they should go up first, that they might not announce beforehand that He was coming, “When His brethren were gone up, then went He also up to the feast day, not openly, but as it were in secret.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “as it were in secret”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He acts there as if in secret.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “as it were in secret”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because neither was this really in secret.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He did not really make an effort to be concealed, who had it in His Own power when He would be taken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in that concealment, as I have said, He gave His weak disciples, who had not the power to prevent being taken when they would not, an example of being on their guard against the snares of enemies.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He went up afterwards even openly, and taught them in the temple; and some said, “‘ Lo, this is He; lo, He is teaching.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Certainly our rulers said that they wished to apprehend Him: ‘Lo, He speaketh openly, and no one layeth hands on Him.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now if we turn our attention to ourselves, if we think of His Body, how that we are even He.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if we were not He, “Forasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of Mine, ye have done it unto Me,” would not be true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we were not He, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou Me?” would not be true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then we are He, in that we are His members, in that we are His Body, in that He is our Head, in that Whole Christ is both Head and Body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure then He foresaw us that we were not to keep the feast days of the Jews, and this is, “I go not up to this feast day.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See neither Christ nor the Evangelist lied; of the which two if one must needs choose one, the Evangelist would pardon me, I would by no means put him that is true before the Truth Himself; I would not prefer him that was sent to Him by whom he was sent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But God be thanked, in my judgment what was obscure has been laid open.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Your piety will aid me before God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold, I have, as I was best able, resolved the question, both concerning Christ and the Evangelist.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hold fast the truth with me as men who love it, embrace charity without contention.
